landing.badge: Free to use · No card required
landing.heroTitle: Every subscription. Every policy. One calm overview.
landing.heroSubtitle: Stop losing track of what you pay for — and what protects you. Hugo brings your subscriptions and insurance into one organised, calm workspace.
landing.featuresTitle: Everything you need, nothing you don't
landing.featuresSubtitle: Built for people who want clarity, not complexity.
landing.featureSmartImportDesc: Connect Gmail or Outlook and let AI detect your subscriptions from receipts and billing emails automatically.
landing.featureInsuranceDesc: Track all your policies, upload documents, and let AI find coverage gaps and overlaps.
landing.featureRenewalDesc: Get notified before subscriptions renew or trials convert to paid. No more surprise charges.
landing.featureAnalyticsDesc: See exactly where your money goes with category breakdowns, trends, and monthly comparisons.
landing.featureClaimsDesc: Log insurance claims from incident to resolution. Track status, reference numbers, and payout amounts all in one place.
landing.featureProposalsDesc: Save insurance quotes and compare premiums side by side before you commit. Track validity dates so you never miss a deadline.
landing.featureCancelDesc: When you remove a subscription, Hugo shows you a direct link to the service's cancellation page.
landing.ctaStorySubtitle: Hugo was built by a parent who wanted a clearer view of the family's finances. Named after their child, it's a reminder that financial clarity isn't just about money — it's about the people it protects.
landing.emailImportSubtitle: Connect Gmail or Outlook and let AI scan your receipts and billing emails. In seconds it detects your recurring subscriptions — you review and approve. No manual entry needed.
landing.gmailDesc: Connect your Google account. Hugo searches your last 6 months of Gmail for billing-related emails and surfaces your active subscriptions.
landing.outlookDesc: Connect your Microsoft account. Hugo searches your Outlook, Hotmail, or Live inbox for receipts and billing emails to detect recurring charges.
landing.privacyReadOnly: Read-only access — we never send or delete emails
landing.privacyAI: AI parsing — no human ever sees your inbox
landing.howItWorksSubtitle: Three steps. No integrations. No bank access required.
landing.step1Desc: Sign up with just your email. Free, no credit card.
landing.step2Desc: Add your subscriptions and insurance policies. Each lives in its own module with analytics, billing, claims, and more.
landing.step3Desc: Your dashboard shows combined totals, coverage insights, and upcoming renewals.
landing.ctaSubtitle: It takes less than two minutes to get started.

pricing.title: Free. Completely.
pricing.subtitle: Hugo is free to use with no hidden tiers, no feature paywalls, and no expiry. Every feature — subscriptions and insurance — is available to every user from day one.
pricing.whyFreeBody: Hugo was built out of frustration with losing track of recurring charges — not as a business model. Keeping it free removes the barrier to actually using it. If Hugo ever introduces a paid tier in the future, all existing features will remain free.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Straight answers to the questions we get asked most.
faq.happyToHelp: We're happy to help — reach out any time.
faq.a1: Yes. Hugo is completely free with no hidden tiers, no feature limits, and no expiry. Every feature — including Gmail import, Outlook import, insurance tracking, household sharing, and email reminders — is available to all users at no cost.
faq.q2: Do I need to connect my email?
faq.a2: No. Email import is optional. You can add every subscription manually in under a minute each. The Gmail and Outlook integrations exist to save time — they're never required.
faq.q3: What happens to my emails when I run an import?
faq.a3: Your emails are read into memory, parsed by AI, and then immediately discarded. Email content is never written to our database or logs. The only thing saved is the list of subscriptions you explicitly approve. You can read the full explanation on the Gmail or Outlook integration pages.
faq.a4: Gmail (Google accounts) and Outlook (Microsoft accounts — including Hotmail and Live). Both use read-only OAuth access, so Hugo can never send, delete, or modify any email.
faq.q5: Does Hugo need access to my bank account?
faq.a5: No. Hugo never connects to any bank or financial institution. You enter subscription amounts manually, or import them from billing emails. That's it.
faq.q6: How accurate is the AI import?
faq.a6: It's good at spotting clear billing emails — receipts, invoices, renewal notices — but not perfect. It may miss subscriptions that don't send formatted receipts, or occasionally suggest something that isn't a recurring charge. That's why every result goes through a review step where you approve or reject each item before anything is saved.
faq.a7: Hugo supports any currency. Each subscription can be tracked in its original currency, and your dashboard converts everything to a single display currency using live exchange rates. You can set your preferred display currency in your account settings.
faq.q8: Can I share subscriptions with my partner or family?
faq.a8: Yes. Hugo has household sharing — one person creates a household, invites others by email, and shared subscriptions and insurance policies appear in everyone's dashboard. Each member can also have their own private items that stay separate.
faq.q9: Will I get notified before a subscription renews?
faq.a9: Yes. Hugo sends email reminders 7 days before any subscription renewal date. If a subscription has a trial end date, you'll also get an alert 3 days before the trial converts to paid. Reminders can be turned off in account settings.
faq.q10: How does price change detection work?
faq.a10: When you run a Gmail or Outlook import scan, Hugo compares the detected amounts against your existing subscriptions. If the name matches but the price is different, it flags it as a potential price change — showing old vs new price — so you can decide whether to accept the update.
faq.a11: Yes. You can permanently delete your account and all associated data from the dashboard settings at any time. Deletion is immediate and irreversible — there is no recovery period.
faq.a12: Passwords are hashed with bcrypt before storage. OAuth tokens (Gmail/Outlook) are stored encrypted and scoped to read-only access. We use HTTPS for all connections. Email content is never persisted — it's read into memory, processed, and discarded.
faq.a13: Hugo Insurance is a module that lets you track all your insurance policies in one place — health, car, home, life, travel, and more. You can see your total monthly and annual premiums, upload policy documents, and get smart insights about your coverage.
faq.q14: How does overlap and gap detection work?
faq.a14: Hugo automatically analyzes your active insurance policies to find potential issues. If you have two or more policies of the same type (e.g. two home insurance policies), it flags a possible overlap. If you're missing commonly recommended coverage types like health, home, car, or liability insurance, it suggests you consider adding them.

about.title: Built to keep your finances honest.
about.intro: Subscriptions and insurance are designed to be forgotten. Small monthly charges that never feel urgent enough to cancel, policies you haven't reviewed in years — until you add them all up. Hugo exists to make that total impossible to ignore.
about.p1: Most people have no idea what they spend on recurring services and insurance each month. Not because they're careless — but because services make it easy to sign up and hard to keep track. A $9.99 subscription here, overlapping insurance policies there, a family plan that costs more than you remember agreeing to.
about.p2: Hugo is a single place to see everything. Track your subscriptions manually or import them from Gmail and Outlook. Add your insurance policies and let Hugo detect coverage gaps and overlaps. From there, you see what you actually spend each month, what's renewing soon, and where your money goes.
about.p3: No bank connection required. No syncing your financial accounts. No black box that decides what counts. Just a clean, honest view of your subscriptions and insurance — updated when you say so.
about.value1Body: A dashboard that tells you what you spend in two seconds is worth more than a hundred charts. Hugo is deliberately simple.
about.value2Body: Your email is scanned in memory and discarded. Nothing is logged. Nothing is sold. We store the minimum needed and nothing more.
about.value3Body: Hugo is free because subscription tracking shouldn't cost money. There are no paywalled features, no usage limits, and no expiry.
about.ctaTitle: Ready to see your full picture?
about.ctaSubtitle: It takes less than two minutes. No card required.

gmail.title: How Gmail import works
gmail.subtitle: Hugo can scan your Gmail receipts to detect recurring subscriptions automatically. Here's exactly what happens — and what we never touch.
gmail.step1Body: When you click Connect Gmail, you're taken to Google's own sign-in and consent screen. You decide whether to allow access. Hugo requests only the minimum permission required: read-only access to your Gmail messages. We cannot send, delete, or modify anything.
gmail.step2Body: Once connected, clicking Import from Gmail triggers a search of your last 6 months of email for messages with billing-related keywords in the subject — things like receipt, invoice, subscription, renewal, and payment. We fetch a maximum of 50 matching emails.
gmail.step3Title: AI reads and parses the emails
gmail.step3Body: The sender, subject, date, and a short excerpt of each email are sent to an AI model in a single request. The AI identifies recurring subscription charges — service name, amount, billing cycle, and next renewal date. No human at Hugo ever sees your emails. The raw email content is never written to disk or a database.
gmail.step4Title: You review and choose what to import
gmail.step4Body: The AI's suggestions appear in a review panel. Every item is pre-selected, but you can uncheck anything you don't want. Only the subscriptions you explicitly approve are saved to your Hugo account — as ordinary subscription records, identical to ones you'd add manually.
gmail.step5Body: You can revoke Hugo's access to your Gmail at any time by clicking the Gmail button in the dashboard header and choosing Disconnect. This immediately deletes your stored OAuth tokens from our database. You can also revoke access directly from your Google Account at myaccount.google.com/permissions.
gmail.dataTableTitle: What we store — and what we don't
gmail.dataTableSubtitle: Plain answers to what happens to your data.
gmail.row1Why: Needed to call the Gmail API on your behalf when you trigger a scan.
gmail.row2Why: Read into memory during the scan, then discarded immediately.
gmail.row3Why: Only a short excerpt is passed to the AI. Never written to our database.
gmail.row4Why: Shown in the review panel only. Discarded when you close the modal.
gmail.row5Why: Saved as normal subscription records in your account — same as adding manually.
gmail.privacyBody: Your inbox is private. Hugo reads the minimum necessary to detect subscriptions — and nothing more. Email content is never logged, never stored, and never shared with anyone except the AI service used for parsing. You are always in control: the import only runs when you trigger it, and you approve every subscription before it is added to your account.
gmail.ctaSubtitle: Create a free account and connect Gmail in under two minutes.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: At Hugo, we believe your data is yours. We're transparent about how we collect, use, and protect your information. Read on for the full details.
privacy.collectBody: When you use Hugo, we collect: Your email address and hashed password for account access. Subscription details you add (names, amounts, renewal dates, categories, notes). Insurance policy information and documents you upload. Bank transcripts you upload for analysis. Gmail and Outlook OAuth tokens to fetch your billing emails (with your permission). Your language preference and display currency. Email reminder preferences. Household membership data if you join a household. Basic metadata like IP address and browser type.
privacy.useBody: We use your data to: Provide core subscription and insurance tracking. Analyze your Gmail and Outlook emails to detect new subscriptions (only when you trigger an import). Analyze insurance documents and bank transcripts using Claude AI to extract recurring charges and coverage information. Send renewal reminder emails (only if you opt in). Support household sharing by making subscriptions visible to household members. Maintain an audit trail of subscription changes. Provide multi-currency support using live exchange rates. Show spending analytics and insights. Delete your account and data when you request it.
privacy.legalBasisBody: We process your personal data on the following legal grounds: Contractual necessity — We need your account information and subscription data to provide Hugo's core service. Legitimate interest — We process minimal metadata for security monitoring and fraud prevention, in ways that do not override your privacy rights. Consent — Optional features such as email reminders, Gmail/Outlook connections, and AI analysis of documents are processed only when you explicitly enable or trigger them. You can withdraw consent at any time in your settings. Legal compliance — We may process or retain data when required by applicable law.
privacy.thirdPartyBody: We share your data with the following services: Anthropic (Claude AI) — Email content (up to 1500 characters), PDF documents, and bank transcripts are sent to Claude for analysis to detect subscriptions and insurance terms. Anthropic retains this data for 30 days for security purposes. See Anthropic's privacy policy for details. Gmail/Microsoft — We request read-only access to your emails. Your email content is read into memory, analyzed, and immediately discarded. Only the subscriptions you approve are stored. Resend — Your email address, subscription names, amounts, and household names are shared for sending transactional emails (password resets, renewal reminders, household invitations). You control email reminders via your settings. Frankfurter — We use the public Frankfurter API for exchange rates. No personal data is sent. We do not share your personal data with third parties for marketing, advertising, or any purpose other than providing Hugo's core functionality. We will never sell your data.
privacy.cookiesBody: Hugo uses only essential cookies — no advertising, tracking, or analytics cookies of any kind. The only cookie stored is an authentication session cookie: a small, secure, HTTP-only cookie that keeps you signed in. It is automatically deleted when you sign out or your session expires. No third-party ad cookies. No cross-site tracking. No analytics fingerprinting. You can delete cookies at any time via your browser settings. Blocking cookies will prevent you from staying signed in.
privacy.internationalBody: Hugo is operated primarily in the United States. If you are located in the European Economic Area (EEA) or United Kingdom, your data may be transferred to and processed in countries outside your region, including the US. We rely on appropriate safeguards for these transfers: Standard Contractual Clauses approved by the European Commission where applicable. Third-party processors include Anthropic (US), Resend (US), and Google/Microsoft (US), each of whom maintains GDPR-compliant data processing terms.
privacy.securityBody: Your passwords are hashed with bcryptjs before storage. OAuth tokens are stored securely and auto-refresh when expired. All data is transmitted over HTTPS/TLS encryption. Uploaded documents are stored on secure servers segregated by user. You can only access your own data. We maintain error logging and monitoring for security issues.
privacy.childrenBody: Hugo is not intended for children under the age of 16. We do not knowingly collect personal data from anyone under 16. If you believe a child under 16 has created an account, please contact privacy@hugo.example.com and we will promptly delete the account and all associated data.
privacy.rightsBody: You have the right to access all your data. You can download your account information from your settings. You have the right to delete your account and all data at any time. Visit Settings → Delete Account or email privacy@hugo.example.com. You have the right to correct your information — update it directly in the app. You have the right to data portability — export your subscriptions and insurance data from Settings. You have the right to opt-out of marketing emails (Hugo only sends transactional emails; toggle email reminders in settings). You have the right to disconnect Gmail/Outlook at any time — this immediately revokes our access and deletes stored OAuth tokens.
privacy.retentionBody: Account data: Kept while your account is active; deleted when you delete your account. OAuth tokens: Kept for convenience; revoked when you disconnect. Password reset tokens: Valid for 15 minutes only. Household invitations: Valid for 7 days; not stored after expiration. Subscription history: Retained for audit trail; deleted when the subscription is deleted. Uploaded files: Deleted from disk when you delete your account or the file explicitly. Email logs: Not stored by Hugo (managed by Resend). Database backups: Retained for 30 days for disaster recovery.
privacy.contactBody: Have questions about your privacy? Email privacy@hugo.example.com. Need to request your data or delete your account? Use Settings → Delete Account or email privacy@hugo.example.com.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.agreementBody: By using Hugo, you agree to these terms. If you don't agree with any part, please don't use the service. Hugo may update these terms at any time. Material changes will be notified via email. Your continued use of Hugo after updates means you accept the new terms.
terms.eligibilityBody: You must be at least 16 years old to use Hugo. By creating an account, you confirm you meet this requirement. Users under 18 should use Hugo with parental or guardian awareness. You must provide accurate and truthful information when registering. You are responsible for keeping your login credentials secure and for all activity on your account. You may only hold one account per person.
terms.useLicenseBody: Hugo is a personal financial tracking tool. You may: Track your own subscriptions and insurance policies. Import subscription data from your Gmail or Outlook inbox. Upload documents for analysis. Share subscriptions and policies with household members via Hugo's household feature. Delete your account at any time. You may not: Attempt to gain unauthorized access to Hugo or its systems. Reverse engineer, decompile, or attempt to derive the source code. Use automated tools or scrapers to extract data. Impersonate other users or create false accounts. Use Hugo for illegal purposes or to violate any law.
terms.userDataBody: You retain full ownership of all data you enter into Hugo — your subscriptions, insurance policies, uploaded documents, and financial information. Hugo does not claim any ownership of your data. You grant Hugo a limited license to store, process, and display your data solely to provide the service. This license ends when you delete your account. You can export your data at any time via Settings. You can permanently delete all your data via Settings → Delete Account.
terms.intellectualPropertyBody: The Hugo platform — including its software, design, interface, logos, and written content — is owned by Hugo and protected under copyright and applicable intellectual property law. You are granted a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable license to use Hugo for personal financial tracking as permitted by these terms. You may not copy, modify, distribute, sell, or sublicense any part of the Hugo platform. The name Hugo and related logos are trademarks and may not be used without written permission.
terms.disclaimerBody: Hugo is provided as-is without warranties of any kind. We do not guarantee: Uptime or continuous availability. Accuracy of AI analysis or subscription detection. Real-time accuracy of exchange rates. That Hugo is error-free or free of harmful code. AI-powered insights are for informational purposes only and are not substitutes for professional financial or insurance advice.
terms.liabilityBody: Hugo is not liable for: Indirect, incidental, or consequential damages (lost savings, missed subscription cancellations, etc.). Errors or omissions in AI analysis. Inaccurate exchange rates or pricing. Service downtime or data loss. Reliance on information provided by Hugo. The maximum liability of Hugo is limited to fees paid in the past 12 months (currently: $0, as Hugo is free). These limitations apply to the fullest extent permitted by law.
terms.indemnificationBody: You agree to indemnify and hold Hugo harmless from any claims, damages, or costs arising from: Your use of Hugo in violation of these terms. Your violation of any law or third-party rights. Your unauthorized access to Hugo or misuse of your account. Content you input into Hugo.
terms.disputeBody: If you have a dispute with Hugo, please contact us first at legal@hugo.example.com with a description of the issue and your desired outcome. We will make a good-faith effort to resolve it within 30 days. If informal resolution fails, disputes will be resolved by binding individual arbitration under the rules of the American Arbitration Association. You and Hugo each waive the right to participate in class action lawsuits or class-wide arbitration. Nothing in this clause prevents you from bringing individual claims in small claims court.
terms.modificationsBody: Hugo may modify or discontinue features at any time, with or without notice. We may terminate your account if: You violate these terms. Your account poses a security risk. The account is inactive for an extended period. You request account deletion.
terms.terminationBody: You may delete your account at any time via Settings → Delete Account. When an account is deleted, all associated data is permanently removed from Hugo's database. See the Privacy Policy for file retention details. Deletion is immediate and irreversible.
terms.governingLawBody: These terms are governed by the laws of the United States. Any legal disputes must be brought in the appropriate courts of the United States. You waive your right to jury trial to the fullest extent permitted by law.
terms.contactBody: Questions about these terms? Email legal@hugo.example.com. For support issues, email support@hugo.example.com.
